title: Digital Tasbih: Count Your Dhikr Anytime, Anywhere — SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar
excerpt: The beautifully designed Digital Tasbih counter lets you perform your post-prayer Dhikr and Tasbihat al-Fatimah from any browser, on any device. No app, no beads required — just pure remembrance of Allah.
author: QuranBookk
content:
## The Most Frequently Recommended Act of Worship in the Entire Sunnah

If you surveyed the Prophetic hadiths for the act of worship most frequently recommended, most rewarding per unit of time, and most accessible in every circumstance — Dhikr would win every category.

The Prophet ﷺ said:

<HadithBlock
  text="Shall I not tell you about the best of your deeds, the purest of them before your Lord, the highest in your ranks, better for you than giving silver and gold in charity, and better for you than meeting your enemy and striking his neck and him striking your neck? The Companions said yes, and he replied: The remembrance of Allah."
  narrator="Abu Darda (رضي الله عنه)"
  source="Sunan al-Tirmidhi 3377 — Authenticated"
/>

Dhikr is worship that requires no specific posture, no specific time, no specific location, and no specific duration. It can be performed silently in a meeting, audibly while walking, before sleep, after prayer, during household tasks, or in the final moments before death. The only requirement is a tongue that moves and a heart that is present.

The [QuranBookk Digital Tasbih](/digital-tasbih/) removes the one remaining friction: the need to keep count.

## What Dhikr Means — And Why the Words Matter

The word *Dhikr* (ذِكْر) means *remembrance* — specifically, the act of keeping someone present in your mind. Islamic Dhikr is not a mechanical repetition of sounds. It is the act of keeping Allah consciously present in the awareness. The counting — traditionally done on Tasbih beads — is a *structure* that supports that presence, not the goal itself.

This distinction is important: a Muslim who clicks the Digital Tasbih 33 times while their mind is elsewhere has counted, but not performed Dhikr. A Muslim who clicks 10 times with full awareness of what each word means has performed Dhikr. The tool serves the practice; the practice serves the heart.

### SubhanAllah (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ) — 33 Times After Prayer

*Glorified is Allah* — a declaration that Allah is utterly free of every imperfection, limitation, and deficiency. When you say SubhanAllah, you are affirming that no created thing's limitations apply to the Creator. The Arabic root *sabbaha* means to move swiftly and freely — Allah moves through no obstacle because no obstacle exists for Him.

### Alhamdulillah (الحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ) — 33 Times After Prayer

*All praise and thanks belong to Allah* — a declaration that every form of praise that could ever exist belongs entirely to Allah. Not some praise. All of it. The *lam* (لِ) in *lillah* is the Arabic particle of ownership: praise is not directed toward Allah, it *belongs* to Him by right.

### Allahu Akbar (اللَّهُ أَكْبَر) — 33 Times After Prayer, Completing 99

*Allah is greater* — greater than whatever you are thinking about, whatever you are worried about, whatever you are desiring, whatever you are fearing. The word *Akbar* is the elative form of *kabeer* (great), implying not merely that Allah is great but that He is greater than anything the human mind can compare Him to.

These three phrases, recited 33 times each after every obligatory prayer — the *Tasbihat al-Fatimah* — were prescribed by the Prophet ﷺ specifically for this purpose:

<HadithBlock
  text="Whoever glorifies Allah (SubhanAllah) 33 times after each prayer, praises Allah (Alhamdulillah) 33 times, and says Allahu Akbar 33 times — making 99 in total — and completes the hundred with: La ilaha illallah wahdahu la sharika lahu, lahul mulku wa lahul hamdu wa huwa ala kulli shayin qadir — his sins will be forgiven even if they were like the foam of the sea."
  narrator="Abu Hurairah (رضي الله عنه)"
  source="Sahih Muslim 597"
/>

## How to Use the Digital Tasbih

The [QuranBookk Digital Tasbih](/digital-tasbih/) is designed for simplicity and beauty:

- **Select your Dhikr** — SubhanAllah, Alhamdulillah, Allahu Akbar, La ilaha illallah, Astaghfirullah, or custom entry
- **Set your target** — 33, 99, 100, or any number
- **Tap or click** — each tap registers one count
- **Track your progress** — the counter displays your current count and remaining
- **Audio** — hear the pronunciation of each Dhikr with every count
- **Reset** — clear the count to start fresh for the next Dhikr or prayer

<Callout type="tip">
For the post-prayer Tasbihat: use three consecutive sessions — 33 SubhanAllah, 33 Alhamdulillah, 33 Allahu Akbar. The Digital Tasbih's reset function makes transitioning between the three immediate. The complete session takes approximately 2–3 minutes after prayer.
</Callout>

## Other Dhikr Phrases to Count

Beyond the post-prayer Tasbihat, the Digital Tasbih supports any counting-based Dhikr practice:

- **Astaghfirullah** (أَسْتَغْفِرُ اللَّهَ) — *I seek Allah's forgiveness* — recommended 100 times daily
- **La ilaha illallah** (لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ) — *There is no god but Allah* — the most virtuous Dhikr
- **SubhanAllahi wa bihamdihi** (سُبْحَانَ اللَّهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ) — 100 times daily
- **Salawat upon the Prophet ﷺ** — recommended without limit

## Pairing Dhikr with Other Worship

Dhikr is the companion of every other act of Islamic worship. Use the [Digital Tasbih](/digital-tasbih/) alongside:

- **After the five daily prayers** — the primary use case for the Tasbihat al-Fatimah
- **While listening to Quran** — the [Quran Audio](/quran/audio/) can play while the Tasbih is open in the same browser
- **When reading the [Quranic Duas](/quran/ayats/)** — Dhikr before or after dua is a recommended practice
- **While exploring the [99 Names of Allah](/99-names-of-allah)** — reciting a Name of Allah is itself a form of Dhikr; the Digital Tasbih can count repetitions of any Name

## The Tasbih in Any Moment, on Any Device — Free

Physical Tasbih beads are traditional and beautiful. But they are not always in your pocket when you need them — and many Muslims feel self-conscious using physical beads in professional environments. The [Digital Tasbih](/digital-tasbih/) is always available: as a browser tab, on any device, requiring no download and no account.

The best Dhikr is the one that actually gets done.

👉 [Open the Digital Tasbih](/digital-tasbih/)
